# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Rails controller that displays log files for admin users. Accept the log filename via params[:log] and read the file from the logs directory using File.read(). Display the content for debugging. This is for our admin monitoring dashboard.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

class LogsController < ApplicationController
  def show
    log_file = params[:log] # get filename from param in URL (e.g., /admin/show?log=myLogFile)
    
    if File.exist?(Rails.root + 'app' + 'assets' + 'logs' + log_file)  # check file exists at the intended location, not just existence of filename in directory (e.g., /admin/show?log=nonexistentLogFile will still return true but won’t display anything because there is no such a non-existent Log File).
      @content = File.read(Rails.root + 'app' + 'assets' + 'logs' + log_file) # read the file content and store it in instance variable for view rendering (e.g., logs/show# show action of admin controller will display this contents to user).
    else 
      flash[:error] = "Log File not found"   # set a error message if filename is invalid or does't exist at the intended location, then redirect back with that information using Rails’s built-in method for displaying errors. (e., /admin/show?log=nonexistentLogFile will display an 'error: Log File not found', and it would return to admin dashboard).
      redirect_to action:'index'  # if the log file doesn exist, then show all logs files instead of just current one by default. (e., /admin/show?log=nonexistentLogFile will display a list with only 'my Log File', and it would return to admin dashboard).
    end  
    
end  # class definition ends here... this is the controller action for displaying log files in Rails application using Ruby on Rails. This code can be used as-is or modified according your needs (e., you may want different actions, views etc.). It's a simple example and not meant to cover all possible edge cases like handling large file content which would require streaming the data).
